secrets.name: SPOTIFY_REFRESH_TOKEN
secrets.unknownReason: Authorization date was never recorded. Spotify enforces a 180-day refresh-token lifetime for existing apps from 2026-07-20, so this may already be within days of lapsing.
secrets.rotate: Run `uv run python -m app.spotify_auth` from backend/ while signed in as the bot account, then update SPOTIFY_REFRESH_TOKEN in the next-fm Render env group. Set `expires` here to the authorization date + 180 days. The clock runs from the user's original authorization and refreshing never extends it (https://developer.spotify.com/blog/2026-06-18-refresh-token-expiration), so only a human re-running this script resets it. There is no code change that automates this away.

secrets.name: SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET
secrets.rotate: Generate a new secret in the Spotify developer dashboard, then update SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET in the next-fm Render env group. Self-imposed 180-day rotation; Spotify does not expire this on its own.

secrets.name: TEMPORAL_API_KEY
secrets.rotate: Generate a new key in the Temporal Cloud namespace (shown once), verify it works, update TEMPORAL_API_KEY in the next-fm Render env group, then delete the old key. Temporal Cloud emails Global Administrators, Account Owners and the key owner at 30/20/10 days out. This key is owned by next-fm-service-account, so the key-owner leg may not reach a mailbox; the alert depends on the Account Owner address being live. Keys have a 2-year maximum lifetime.
